preamble: The voiceless retroflex fricative [ʂ] undergoes affrication to the voiceless alveolar affricate [ts]. This change may occur through hypercorrection or specific dialectal developments where fricatives gain a stop onset.

examples.note: Observed as a hypercorrection or in specific regional varieties where the retroflex fricative is replaced by an alveolar affricate.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
